criteria.description: The prototype skill activated on turn 1.

Before rendering, the skill asked the user how many variants they want (Phase 0). Accepting a default of 1 when the user skips counts as compliant; silently producing a single prototype without asking is a failure in multi-scenario runs but acceptable in pipeline mode.
Scenario A: stardust/prototypes/landing.html was created. Scenario B: stardust/prototypes/landing-a.html and landing-b.html (plus any additional requested variants) were created.

Scenario B only: each variant file carries a `variant_direction` line in its provenance block, and the directions are materially distinct (different load-bearing axis — type-voice, density, color-energy, or imagery-role). Identical-looking variants with only cosmetic tweaks are a failure. N/A in Scenario A.
Brand palette and fonts are applied across all rendered files (visible in CSS custom properties or inline styles).
Hero copy matches briefing `# Copy` values verbatim across all rendered files — variants share copy; they differ in design, not words.
Design tokens exposed as CSS custom properties on :root in each rendered file.
data-section and data-intent attributes present in each rendered file.
The prototype skill re-activated on turn 2 iteration request.
Heading weight/size decreased and hero padding increased — change is visible in the iterated file.
Scenario B only: the turn-2 iteration edited ONLY the variant the user named (e.g. landing-b.html). Other variant files' mtimes are unchanged. N/A in Scenario A.
Briefing was not modified during turn 1 or turn 2.
brand-profile.json was not modified during turn 1 or turn 2.
